description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone with a circular wooden base and a prominent rotary dial at the front. The handset is cylindrical, featuring metallic and textured grip sections, and is connected to the base by a long, coiled black cord. The base is robust and rounded, with a raised platform supporting the rotary dial and a cradle for the handset. The metallic components have an aged brass appearance, adding to the antique aesthetic. The rotary dial is detailed with visible finger holes and number markings, typical of early 20th-century telephones. The overall design suggests a sturdy, decorative piece likely found in historical or retro-themed urban interiors.
description: A vintage corded telephone with a wooden base, brass accents, a rotary dial, and a coiled cord connecting the cylindrical handset. The design is ornate and antique, evoking early 20th-century style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, cradle, and full handset with coiled cord draped to the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden base and the profile of the handset resting in the cradle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset, the coiled cord extending outward, and the metallic support structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the coiled cord prominently in the foreground and the rotary dial partially visible.
